$comment: Phase 7.F.5 session 103 closure: KAT golden vectors for v2.0.0 dual-signature cross-language byte-equivalence. SOURCE OF TRUTH is packages/evidence/src/v2-dual-sig-kats.test.ts (matching the audit-log KAT pattern at packages/evidence/tests/audit-log-export.test.ts:564-650). This file pins the byte-precise outputs Go-side primitives MUST recompute exactly. If any value below drifts from the TS golden vector, the cross-language contract has BROKEN and the cause must be investigated — NOT just snapshot-updated. Per spec §18.3 (FIPS 204 deterministic-variant) + §18.4 (canonical SPKI byte construction + signature byte format).

kats_skipped.v2-signature.sig JCS canonicalization: WRITER-SIDE primitive; Go verifier consumes signature.sig as bytes-as-loaded (no recanonicalization at verify-time per check1_v2_dual_sig.go bytes-as-loaded posture). Cross-language byte-equivalence at the JCS-canonical-bytes layer is covered transitively by the 27-fixture conformance suite (Go verifier reads TS-emitted bundle.zips and Check 1 verifies; if JCS canonicalize diverged TS↔Go, the signatures wouldn't verify).
kats_skipped.v2-manifest.json JCS canonicalization: Same rationale as signature.sig — writer-side primitive; verifier consumes raw bytes. Conformance fixtures cover transitively.
kats_skipped.Ed25519 SPKI wrapping: Go uses x509.MarshalPKIXPublicKey + x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey via stdlib (well-tested across Go versions). TS uses Node createPrivateKey/createPublicKey.export({format:'der',type:'spki'}). Both produce RFC 5280-conformant Ed25519 SPKI; no cross-language byte-drift risk warranting a KAT vector here (in contrast to ML-DSA-65 SPKI which requires custom byte-construction per spec §18.4).

pinned_inputs.test_seed_hex_comment: Synthesized 32-byte test seed (bytes 0x00-0x1F in sequence; intentionally non-random + obviously test-vector). Both Ed25519 (RFC 8032 §5.1.5) + ML-DSA-65 (FIPS 204 §6.2) take 32-byte seeds; same seed bytes generate ALGORITHMICALLY DISTINCT keypairs.
pinned_inputs.test_message_hex_comment: Synthesized 32-byte test message. Pinned to enable both TS + Go to derive byte-identical deterministic signatures per RFC 8032 §5.1.6 (Ed25519) + FIPS 204 §6.2 deterministic-variant (ML-DSA-65).

KAT-V2-1.description: ML-DSA-65 keygen-from-seed: derives the 1952-byte raw public key + 1974-byte canonical SPKI DER per spec §18.4. TS computes via @noble/post-quantum keygenFromSeed + wrapPublicKeySpki + encodeSpkiB64. Go computes via cloudflare/circl mldsa65.NewKeyFromSeed + MarshalBinary(publicKey) + keys.MlDsa65SPKIPrefix concatenation. Cross-language byte-equivalence: SHA-256 of raw pubkey + SHA-256 of SPKI MUST match exactly.
KAT-V2-1.expected_spki_prefix_first22_hex_comment: The 22-byte canonical SPKI prefix per spec §18.4 worked construction. MUST match keys.MlDsa65SPKIPrefix byte-for-byte (any drift = cross-language SPKI divergence).

KAT-V2-2.description: ML-DSA-65 FIPS 204 §6.2 deterministic-variant signature over TEST_MESSAGE. TS computes via signDeterministic(message, secretKey). Go computes via mldsa65.SignTo(sk, msg, nil, false, sig) where the 4th arg false = randomized=false = deterministic-variant. Spec §18.3 pins deterministic-variant for v2.0.0 (rnd=32 zero bytes; ctx=empty). Cross-language byte-equivalence: the SHA-256 of the 3309-byte signature MUST match exactly.
KAT-V2-2.expected_sig_byte_length_comment: FIPS 204 §4 Table 1 final (August 2024) mandates ML-DSA-65 signatures are EXACTLY 3309 bytes. Pre-finalization Dilithium3 academic implementations produced 3293 bytes (spec §18.4 line 2369 documents this as a non-conformance vector).

KAT-V2-3.description: Ed25519 deterministic signature per RFC 8032 §5.1.6 over TEST_MESSAGE using a key derived from TEST_SEED. TS derives via Node createPrivateKey({key: pkcs8DerWithSeed, format:'der', type:'pkcs8'}) + cryptoSign(null, msg, key). Go derives via ed25519.NewKeyFromSeed(seed) + ed25519.Sign(privKey, msg). Cross-language byte-equivalence: the 64-byte signature hex MUST match exactly.
KAT-V2-3.expected_pub_spki_hex_comment: 44-byte RFC 5280 SPKI DER for Ed25519 (12-byte AlgorithmIdentifier prefix + 32-byte raw public key).
